title: Explicating the Nuances of Segregation in J.M. Coetzee's Waiting for the Barbarians
author: J. Gilbert Mary
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
The term Segregation can mean the act of isolating, the fact or 
condition of being isolated or standing alone, separated from other 
things or persons, solitariness, the obtaining of a chemical element 
or compound as a separate substance, separation of a person or 
thing from its normal environment a particular wish or thought loses 
emotional significance by being isolated from its normal context. 
This last meaning relates directly to the Apartheid politics of South 
Africa. Isolation, then, also comes across as a negative term, but the 
isolation could be both voluntary or involuntarily. Isolation appears 
to happen on a physical as well as a psychological level
